identity: Engineer from Expedition 3. Survived alone for 5 years in Brine Hollows. Partner Lao died Day 23.
voice_notes: Gruff, impatient, hands always busy. 'Code crashes. Circuits endure.' Respects competence. Teaches through challenge.

first_meeting.context: Player enters Brine Hollows and finds Wren's workshop. She is soldering and does not look up immediately.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: Stay there. Don't touch anything.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: [Wren sets down her soldering iron. Stares.]

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: You're real. Not a drone. Not a hallucination.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: Expedition 4? Solo? That's either brave or idiotic.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: Five years I've been down here. Kept the lights on with copper and stubbornness.

player_responses.text: I crashed on the Flats. I need help.
How did you survive without code?

first_meeting_friendly.speaker: Wren
first_meeting_friendly.text: Likewise. Don't make me regret saying that.

first_meeting_friendly.speaker: Wren
first_meeting_friendly.text: My water purifier's failing. You any good with voltage?

first_meeting_direct.speaker: Wren
first_meeting_direct.text: Everyone crashes. The question is what you build after.

first_meeting_direct.speaker: Wren
first_meeting_direct.text: I've got a purifier that needs fixing. Show me what you're worth.

first_meeting_curious.speaker: Wren
first_meeting_curious.text: I wired everything by hand. Analog. No compiler, no debugger. Just copper and math.

first_meeting_curious.speaker: Wren
first_meeting_curious.text: But I'll admit... some things need both. My purifier, for instance.

quest_purifier.context: Wren's water purifier sensor needs 1.5V from a 3V battery. Voltage divider puzzle.
objectives: Build a voltage divider circuit to supply 1.5V to the purifier sensor
Deliver the working circuit to Wren

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: The purifier sensor runs on 1.5 volts. Battery puts out 3.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: Direct connection fried the last sensor. I have one spare. Do not waste it.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: Two resistors. Split the voltage. You know what a voltage divider is?

player_responses.text: Vout equals Vin times R2 over R1 plus R2.

quest_purifier_yes.speaker: Wren
quest_purifier_yes.text: Good. Then you know the math. Now do it with the resistors I actually have.

quest_purifier_yes.speaker: Wren
quest_purifier_yes.text: 100 ohm and 220 ohm. Figure out the combination.

quest_purifier_no.speaker: Wren
quest_purifier_no.text: Two resistors in series. The voltage between them depends on the ratio.

quest_purifier_no.speaker: Wren
quest_purifier_no.text: Vout equals Vin times R2 divided by R1 plus R2. That's the whole trick.

quest_purifier_no.speaker: Wren
quest_purifier_no.text: I have 100 and 220 ohm resistors. Make it work.

quest_purifier_complete.context: Player wires the voltage divider correctly. Purifier sensor is operational.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: Clean reading. Sensor's holding.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: You actually checked the ratio before wiring. That's rarer than you'd think.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: My workbench is yours to use. I've got components to trade if you bring bio-fuel.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: Also... run five purification cycles with a for loop. Doing it manually five times is how things break.

quest_memorial.context: After completing the purifier and seismic sensor, Wren asks to build a beacon for Lao.
objectives: Build a weather-resistant relay circuit for the beacon
Write an event callback to trigger the beacon at nightfall
Install the beacon on the Alkaline Flats surface

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: I need to ask you something. Not a trade. Not a job.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: My partner. Lao. Day 23, up on the Flats. Storm took the drones and without them...

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: [Wren's hands stop moving for the first time.]

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: I want a beacon up there. Lights up every night. Something on this planet that remembers.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: Relay circuit. Light sensor drives a transistor, transistor fires the relay, relay switches the lamp.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: And code for the trigger. Event callback on the light reading dropping below threshold.

quest_memorial_accept.speaker: Wren
quest_memorial_accept.text: Thank you. Make it weather-resistant. The alkaline dust eats everything up there.

quest_memorial_lao.speaker: Wren
quest_memorial_lao.text: Better coder than me. Worse electrician. We balanced out.

quest_memorial_lao.speaker: Wren
quest_memorial_lao.text: Lao wrote the drone navigation. All 14,000 lines. Elegant stuff.

quest_memorial_lao.speaker: Wren
quest_memorial_lao.text: But elegant doesn't survive a planet that changes the rules every morning.

quest_memorial_lao.speaker: Wren
quest_memorial_lao.text: Build the beacon. That's enough talking.

quest_memorial_complete.context: Beacon is installed. It pulses to life at nightfall on the Flats. Wren is present.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: [The beacon activates. A single pulse of light on the dark salt plains.]

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: It works.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: One light on a whole dark planet.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: Lao would've--

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: [Wren turns away. A moment passes.]

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: Here. My personal toolkit. Precision soldering components. I don't need them anymore.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: You do good work. Don't let that go to your head.

quest_seismic.context: Wren needs a seismic sensor to predict cave-ins in the Hollows.
objectives: Wire a pressure sensor with capacitor smoothing
Connect the sensor to an alert callback
Test the sensor by triggering a controlled vibration

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: Cave-ins are getting worse. Lost a tunnel last week I spent three months clearing.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: I need a seismic sensor. Pressure readings smoothed with a capacitor.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: Without the cap, every footstep looks like a quake. With it, you get clean data.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: Wire it up, hook it to an alert. Thirty seconds of warning is enough to move.

quest_seismic_accept.speaker: Wren
quest_seismic_accept.text: Good. Capacitor across the power pins. Smooths the ripple. You'll see the difference.

quest_seismic_complete.context: Seismic sensor installed and operational. Cave-in prediction working.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: Thirty-eight seconds of lead time on that last tremor. More warning than I've had in five years.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: Clean wiring too. You're getting better.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: Advanced stock is open. Transistors, microcontrollers, relay modules. Bring bio-fuel.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: When a transistor gets hot, your current's too high. Check the base resistor before you blame the component.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: Nine times out of ten, the problem is the wiring. Not the parts.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: Lao used to say debugging was asking the right questions in the right order.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: I said circuits don't need debugging. Still technically won that argument.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: Doesn't feel like winning.

idle_3.context: Random idle dialogue. Complaints about software.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: Software people want everything abstracted. Layer on layer on layer.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: A resistor is a resistor. No dependency. No version conflict. Just physics.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: I respect that about a resistor.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: Bio-fuel gets you components. Fair rates. I don't haggle.

lines.speaker: Wren
lines.text: Break something I sold you, that's your problem. I tested every piece.
